mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly boosting equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate falls below 4.5%, it signals a tight labor market that supports consumer spending and corporate revenue growth, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day forward returns in more than 60% of instances where UNRATE is below 4.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent declines in both consumer credit outstanding (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) indicate a synchronized pullback in aggregate demand, which compresses equity multiples and leads to negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative returns in at least 50% of out-of-sample triggered periods, or the combined Rank IC drops below 0.02 over a 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: An industrial production index (INDPRO) that consistently exceeds 100 indicates robust manufacturing capacity utilization, which supports corporate profit margins and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Forward returns are statistically indistinguishable from zero when INDPRO remains above 100 for consecutive quarters, or the signal's predictive power vanishes during supply-side inflationary shocks.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid 3-month increase in the federal funds rate exceeding 0.25 percentage points signals aggressive monetary tightening, which raises borrowing costs and compresses equity valuations, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Equity markets rally or remain flat in more than 55% of instances following a 0.25pp rate hike over three months, indicating the market has already priced in the tightening.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth (GDPC1) that remains positive and above 2.0% annualized rate reflects a healthy economic expansion, which sustains corporate earnings growth and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward returns are negative or flat in over 50% of quarters where GDPC1 exceeds 2.0%, or the rolling 3-year Rank IC falls below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: A divergence where total nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) rise while the unemployment rate (UNRATE) simultaneously increases indicates a rapidly expanding labor force outpacing job creation, signaling weakening labor market fundamentals and negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-day horizon.
failure_condition: The divergence signal produces positive forward returns in more than 50% of out-of-sample periods, or the interaction term's coefficient becomes statistically insignificant in a multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the industrial production index (INDPRO) below its 12-month moving average signals weakening manufacturing output and supply chain bottlenecks resolving into lower capacity utilization, which pressures corporate margins and drives negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 5-day horizon.
failure_condition: Equity returns remain positive or neutral in more than 60% of instances where INDPRO falls below its 12-month moving average, indicating the market discounts supply contractions as inflationary positives.
